en:
The search for oil has become one of the most expensive gambles in modern industry.
Geologists study rock formations and analyse seismic data to identify areas where oil may be trapped.
A single exploratory well can cost millions of dollars and must be drilled thousands of metres deep.
Rock samples taken from the well are analysed in laboratories to determine whether a viable reservoir exists.
Not every promising site yields commercially viable quantities of crude oil.
Large reserves have been located beneath the continental shelf, but reaching them demands advanced technology.
The process from initial exploration to full production often takes many years.
Despite the risks, the rewards of a major discovery continue to drive the global search for oil.
cn:
石油勘探已成为现代工业中最昂贵的赌博之一。
地质学家研究岩层、分析地震数据，以标定可能蕴藏石油的区域。
一口勘探井耗资数百万美元，须钻至数千米深处。
取自油井的岩样在实验室中分析，以判定是否存在可开采的储油层。
并非每个前景看好的地点都能产出具有商业价值的原油。
大陆架下已发现大型储量，但开采需要先进技术。
从初步勘探到全面投产，往往历时数年。
尽管风险巨大，重大发现的可能仍推动着全球的石油勘探。

en:
Chaos theory teaches that tiny changes in initial conditions can produce vastly different outcomes.
Meteorologists have long admitted that long-range weather forecasts are inherently unreliable.
The flap of a butterfly's wings in Brazil might, in theory, set off a tornado in Texas.
This famous "butterfly effect" reveals a deep gap between deterministic equations and unpredictable reality.
If the initial temperature had been even slightly higher, the entire weather pattern would have changed.
Had scientists accepted these limits earlier, billions might have been saved on futile prediction efforts.
Only when the scientific community recognised the chaos in complex systems did the theory gain acceptance.
Science can describe laws of nature, yet it cannot predict everything with precision.
cn:
混沌理论告诉我们，初始条件的微小变化可能产生截然不同的结果。
气象学家早已承认，长期天气预报本质上不可靠。
理论上，巴西一只蝴蝶扇动翅膀，或许会在德克萨斯引发龙卷风。
这一著名的「蝴蝶效应」揭示了确定性方程与不可预测现实之间的深刻鸿沟。
若初始温度略高一点，整个天气模式便会改变。
要是科学家更早接受这些局限，本可节省数十亿徒劳的预测开支。
科学界认识到复杂系统中的混沌之后，该理论才获得认可。
科学能描述自然规律，却无法精确预测一切。

en:
Industrial espionage and the protection of trade secrets are hidden sides of the modern economy.
Large companies invest fortunes in developing new products, yet must guard against rivals stealing their formulas.
Employees who have access to confidential information are often required to sign secrecy agreements.
The fact that secrets are difficult to keep alarms executives across every major industry.
Documents that contain proprietary data are stored in secure vaults with restricted access.
A single breach of confidentiality can cost a company millions of dollars.
There is a risk that careless conversation may leak vital information to competitors.
Companies safeguard their competitive edge with strict internal rules and constant vigilance.
cn:
工业间谍活动与商业秘密的保护，是现代经济中不为人知的一面。
大公司投入巨资研发新产品，却须防范对手窃取配方。
接触机密信息的员工往往须签署保密协议。
秘密难以保守这一事实令各行业高管担忧。
含专有数据的文件存放在限制出入的保险库中。
一次泄密就可能使公司损失数百万美元。
存在因疏忽谈话向竞争对手泄露关键信息的风险。
公司以严格的内部规章和持续警惕来保护竞争优势。

en:
The modern city is humanity's greatest creation and its most persistent headache.
Millions of people flocking to urban centres strain ageing infrastructure beyond its limits.
City planners build subways and ring roads to ease congestion, yet traffic jams persist.
It is the sheer scale of the metropolis that makes it so difficult to manage effectively.
Areas affected by pollution and overcrowding spread outward as cities continue to sprawl.
Zoning laws separate factories from residential districts, but enforcement remains a constant challenge.
It was only in the twentieth century that the metropolis became the dominant form of settlement.
The modern city grows by its own logic, often beyond any planner's blueprint.
cn:
现代城市是人类最伟大的造物，也是最令人头疼的难题。
涌入城市中心的数百万人使老化基础设施不堪重负。
城市规划者修建地铁与环城路以缓解拥堵，交通堵塞却依然存在。
正是大都市的巨大规模使其如此难以有效管理。
随着城市蔓延，受污染与过度拥挤影响的区域不断向外扩展。
分区法规将工厂与居住区隔开，但执法仍是持续挑战。
直到二十世纪，大都市才成为主导聚居形态。
现代城市按自身逻辑生长，往往超出任何规划者的蓝图。

en:
Bovine spongiform encephalopathy, commonly known as mad cow disease, was a man-made disaster.
Cattle were fed processed remains of other animals, and prions spread rapidly through the herds.
The feed supply was contaminated with infected material long before the outbreak was detected.
Millions of cattle were slaughtered to contain an epidemic that devastated British farming.
Humans who consumed infected beef risked developing a fatal brain disease.
If farmers had not changed the animals' diet, the catastrophe might never have occurred.
The practice of using meat-and-bone meal as cattle feed has since been banned.
The tragedy stands as a warning against sacrificing safety for short-term profit.
cn:
牛海绵状脑病，俗称疯牛病，是一场人为灾难。
牛曾被喂食其他动物的加工残骸，朊病毒在牛群中迅速传播。
疫情被发现之前，饲料早已被感染物质污染。
为控制摧毁英国畜牧业的疫情，数百万头牛被屠宰。
食用受感染牛肉的人可能患上致命的脑病。
若农民未改变牛的饲料，这场灾难或许根本不会发生。
用肉骨粉喂牛的做法此后已被禁止。
这场悲剧警示人们：不可为短期利益牺牲安全。

en:
Porpoises are among the most intelligent marine mammals, yet thousands die each year as bycatch in fishing nets.
A pod of porpoises communicates through acoustic signals beyond the range of human hearing.
They navigate by a natural form of sonar, bouncing high-frequency sounds off obstacles in the water.
Several species are now endangered, threatened by pollution and the destruction of their habitat.
If conservation laws had been enforced earlier, countless porpoises would have been saved.
Had I known earlier about the bycatch crisis, I would have helped campaign for safer nets.
Never before had researchers observed such complex social behaviour among these creatures.
Only through international cooperation can we ensure that porpoises continue to thrive in the open sea.
cn:
鼠海豚是最聪慧的海洋哺乳动物之一，却每年数千头死于渔网误捕。
一群鼠海豚通过人耳听不见的声学信号交流。
它们靠天然声呐导航，将高频声波从水中障碍物反弹回来。
若干物种现已濒危，面临污染与栖息地破坏的威胁。
若保护法规更早实施，无数鼠海豚本可获救。
要是我早知道误捕危机，就会帮忙倡导更安全的渔网。
研究者从未观察到这些生物之间如此复杂的社交行为。
唯有通过国际合作，才能确保鼠海豚继续在公海中繁衍生息。

en:
Are dreams an outlet for subconscious desires, or merely a by-product of the brain processing memories?
Freud held the belief that dreams are the royal road to the unconscious mind.
Repressed wishes, he argued, surface in symbolic form during sleep.
Neuroscience offers a different view, linking vivid dreams to REM sleep and brain activity.
There is growing evidence that the brain consolidates memories while we dream.
Images appearing in dreams often reflect anxieties experienced during waking hours.
Studying dreams helps psychologists understand emotions that the conscious mind conceals.
The stuff of dreams remains one of the deepest mysteries of human experience.
cn:
梦究竟是潜意识欲望的宣泄，还是大脑整理记忆的副产品？
弗洛伊德持有这一信念：梦是通往潜意识的皇家大道。
他认为，被压抑的愿望在睡眠中以象征形式浮现。
神经科学提出不同见解，将生动梦境与快速眼动睡眠及脑活动联系起来。
越来越多的证据表明，大脑在梦中巩固记忆。
梦中出现的意象往往反映清醒时的焦虑。
研究梦有助于心理学家理解有意识心灵所隐藏的情感。
梦的内容仍是人类经验中最深奥的谜团之一。

en:
Snake venom is one of nature's most complex biochemical weapons, yet it can also save lives.
The venom contains dozens of toxins, some of which attack the blood and others the nervous system.
Snakes whose venom affects the nervous system are among the most dangerous species on earth.
Useful compounds that have been isolated from venom are used in medicines for heart disease.
Antivenom must be administered quickly after a bite to neutralise the poison.
Milking a snake — collecting its venom — requires skill, courage and steady nerves.
Antivenom serum has been developed for most of the world's deadliest snake species.
What kills in one dose may, in refined form, heal conditions that no other drug can treat.
cn:
蛇毒是自然界最复杂的生化武器之一，却也能救人性命。
毒液含数十种毒素，有的攻击血液，有的攻击神经系统。
毒液影响神经系统的蛇是地球上最危险的物种之一。
从毒液中分离出的有用化合物用于心脏病药物。
被咬后须迅速注射抗蛇毒血清以中和毒素。
取蛇毒——采集毒液——需要技巧、勇气与沉稳。
针对世界上大多数最致命蛇种已研制出抗蛇毒血清。
一剂即可致命之物，经提炼后或可治愈其他药物无法治疗的疾病。

en:
William Shakespeare was born in Stratford-upon-Avon in 1564 and died there in 1616, at the age of fifty-two.
He left the world thirty-seven plays and a hundred and fifty-four sonnets of enduring beauty.
The plays that Shakespeare wrote continue to be performed in every corner of the globe.
By the time he returned to Stratford, he had written most of his greatest tragedies and comedies.
Many of his works were first performed at the Globe Theatre in London.
The Elizabethan era, during which he flourished, was a golden age of English literature.
The authorship controversy, though fierce at times, has done little to diminish his reputation.
Four centuries on, his insight into human nature remains as fresh and powerful as ever.
cn:
威廉·莎士比亚一五六四年生于埃文河畔斯特拉特福，一六一六年在故里去世，享年五十二岁。
他留给世界三十七部戏剧和一百五十四首隽永的十四行诗。
莎士比亚创作的戏剧仍在全球各地上演。
他回到斯特拉特福时，已写完大部分最伟大的悲剧与喜剧。
他的许多作品首演于伦敦环球剧场。
他声名鹊起的伊丽莎白时代，是英国文学的黄金时期。
作者身份争议虽时而激烈，却丝毫未损其声誉。
四个世纪过去，他对人性的洞察依旧鲜活有力。

en:
A returning traveller was stopped at customs with a suspicious quantity of duty-free cigarettes and spirits.
The goods far exceeded the legal limit, and officers accused him of attempting to smuggle them.
He insisted that the packages were gifts from friends and that he had never opened them.
The prosecution failed to prove that he had known what the packages contained.
Had he known the contents, he would have declared them at the border without hesitation.
Little did he suspect that the innocent-looking gifts concealed contraband.
The jury acquitted him, returning a verdict of not guilty on all charges.
The case raised a wider question: how much intent must the law require to establish guilt?
cn:
一位归国旅客在海关被拦下，携带可疑数量的免税香烟与烈酒。
商品远超法定限额，官员指控他企图走私。
他坚称包裹是朋友所赠，从未拆开。
检方未能证明他明知包裹内为何物。
若他知道内容，本会在边境毫不犹豫申报。
他丝毫没料到看似无害的礼物里藏有违禁品。
陪审团裁定他所有指控不成立，宣告无罪。
此案引发更广泛的问题：法律认定有罪究竟需要多大程度的主观故意？

en:
One man's meat is another man's poison — tastes and preferences can never be fully harmonised.
Suet pudding, a traditional British delicacy, is enough to repel many visitors from abroad.
Many English diners have a deep aversion to highly spiced food, while Latin Americans relish it.
Our palate is shaped largely in childhood, and acquired tastes take years to develop.
What one culture enjoys, another may find impossible to appreciate.
Many people prefer familiar dishes to exotic ones, and there is nothing irrational in that.
It takes patience and exposure to cultivate a taste for unfamiliar cuisine.
Respecting differences in food preference may be the most important lesson in cross-cultural understanding.
cn:
各有所爱，各有所恶——口味与偏好永远无法完全统一。
羊油布丁是英国传统佳肴，却足以令许多外国游客却步。
许多英国食客对辛辣食物深感厌恶，拉丁美洲人却乐此不疲。
味觉主要在童年形成，后天养成的口味需多年培养。
一种文化喜爱的东西，另一种文化或许完全无法欣赏。
许多人偏爱熟悉菜肴而非异域风味，这并无不合理之处。
培养对陌生美食的口味需要耐心与接触。
尊重食物偏好的差异，或许是跨文化理解中最重要的一课。

en:
Almost every old family has a skeleton in the cupboard — a secret too shameful to reveal.
An antique dealer who specialised in old furniture made a grisly discovery in a purchased cupboard.
Hidden inside was a human skeleton that the former owners had concealed for generations.
The family that had owned the furniture feared damage to their reputation above all else.
It was inside that old cupboard that the dealer uncovered a scandal buried for decades.
The owner offered to buy back the furniture at any price to prevent the story from spreading.
The cupboard in which the bones had been hidden had not been opened for over fifty years.
The dealer promised to remain discreet, recognising that every family has something it prefers to forget.
cn:
几乎每个古老家族都有「家丑」——羞于外扬的秘密。
一位专营旧家具的古董商在购买的一只橱柜中有了可怕的发现。
里面藏着一具人骨，前任物主世代隐瞒。
曾拥有这件家具的家族最怕名誉受损。
正是在那只旧橱柜里，古董商揭开了埋藏数十年的丑闻。
物主提出不惜代价买回家具，以防事态扩散。
藏有骸骨的橱柜五十多年来未曾打开。
古董商承诺守口如瓶，明白每个家庭都有宁愿遗忘之事。
